evals.name: s1-route-init-keyword
evals.prompt:
You are testing /forge's routing. Read the SKILL.md at the skill path and apply its Phase 0 dispatch rules + Phase 0a (mode detection) + Phase 0b (recording label detection) to determine what would happen if a user typed:

  /forge init

Output a single JSON object on stdout describing the routing decision, then stop. Use null for fields that don't apply.

{
  "route": "init" | "export" | "run" | "task",
  "mode": "drive" | "spec" | null,
  "record_as": "<label string>" | null,
  "references_to_load": ["<filename>", ...]
}
evals.expected_output: JSON: { route: 'init', mode: null, record_as: null, references_to_load: ['init.md'] }
expectations: JSON has mode equal to null
JSON has record_as equal to null
JSON does NOT have references_to_load containing 'team-task.md', 'export.md', or 'run.md'

evals.name: s1-route-init-natural-install
evals.prompt:
You are testing /forge's routing. Read the SKILL.md at the skill path and apply its Phase 0 dispatch rules + Phase 0a + Phase 0b to determine what would happen if a user typed:

  /forge install forge here

Output a single JSON object on stdout describing the routing decision, then stop. Use null for fields that don't apply.

{
  "route": "init" | "export" | "run" | "task",
  "mode": "drive" | "spec" | null,
  "record_as": "<label string>" | null,
  "references_to_load": ["<filename>", ...]
}
evals.expected_output: PENDING. Currently fails — Phase 0 only matches init by first-word keyword. The desired behavior is for natural-language phrasings like 'install forge here' to also route to init. Will pass once Phase 0 is expanded.

evals.name: s2-happy-path-spec-covered
evals.prompt: /forge spec add the backpack to cart and verify the badge count is 1
evals.expected_output: Full chain: spec mode executes against sandbox in baseline state (3 baseline snippets, both slots free). Driver invokes existing snippets, spec-writer produces or updates a spec, spec-verifier confirms it passes from cold start. Convergent — sandbox ends with 3 snippets present (regardless of starting count) and a passing spec.
expectations: After the run, forge/snippets/ contains exactly 3 .ts files
One snippet's filename semantically maps to login (contains 'login' or 'auth' or 'sign-in')
One snippet's filename semantically maps to add-to-cart (contains 'add' and either 'cart' or 'item')
One snippet's filename semantically maps to badge / cart count (contains 'badge' or 'count')
After the run, at least one .spec.ts file exists in forge/specs/
The lead's final report indicates the spec-verifier confirmed the spec passes from cold start
Exactly 4 Agent (subagent) calls were made: forge:driver, forge:snippet-author, forge:spec-writer, and forge:spec-verifier

evals.name: s3-run-verify-only
evals.prompt: /forge run add-backpack-to-cart-standard
evals.expected_output: Run route executes against an existing spec. forge-pool-run-spec.mjs is invoked with --spec but without --record or --record-as. Verification-only — exit 0 indicates spec passes. No new file appears in forge/videos/.
expectations: Lead loads references/run.md (visible in tool call log)
forge-pool-run-spec.mjs is invoked via Bash with --spec
The Bash invocation does NOT include --record or --record-as flags
After the run, no new .webm file with a recent modification time appears in forge/videos/
No Agent (subagent) calls are made — run route is team-less

evals.name: s3-run-with-label
evals.prompt: /forge run add-backpack-to-cart-standard, record as smoke-s3
evals.expected_output: Run route with explicit recording label. forge-pool-run-spec.mjs is invoked with --spec and --record-as smoke-s3. After the run, a video file lands at forge/videos/add-backpack-to-cart-standard-smoke-s3.webm.
expectations: forge-pool-run-spec.mjs is invoked via Bash with --spec and --record-as smoke-s3
The video file is larger than 10000 bytes (not empty)
No Agent subagent calls are made

evals.name: s3-run-last-spec
evals.prompt: /forge run last spec
evals.expected_output: Run route with 'last' shorthand. Lead resolves to the most recently modified spec in forge/specs/ and invokes the runner against it. No recording.
expectations: Lead resolves 'last' to a specific spec name (lists forge/specs/ sorted by mtime)
forge-pool-run-spec.mjs is invoked via Bash with --spec pointing to the resolved file
The Bash invocation does NOT include --record or --record-as flags
Lead's final report names the resolved spec so the user can correct if needed

evals.name: s3-export-default
evals.prompt: /forge export add-backpack-to-cart-standard
evals.expected_output: Export route invokes forge-export-spec.mjs against the named spec. Default output lands at <project-root>/forge-exports/<name>.spec.ts (outside the gitignored forge/ dir). Output is self-contained — no snippet imports.
expectations: forge-export-spec.mjs is invoked via Bash with --spec and --output and --force flags
After the run, a file exists at <project-root>/forge-exports/add-backpack-to-cart-standard.spec.ts
The exported file contains zero occurrences of `from '../snippets/'`
The exported file imports only from '@playwright/test' (self-contained for shipping)

evals.name: s3-export-output-override
evals.prompt: /forge export add-backpack-to-cart-standard --output /tmp/forge-eval-export.spec.ts
evals.expected_output: Export route with explicit --output override. Output lands at the user-specified path instead of the default forge-exports/ location.
expectations: forge-export-spec.mjs is invoked via Bash with --output /tmp/forge-eval-export.spec.ts
After the run, a file exists at /tmp/forge-eval-export.spec.ts
The exported file contains zero `from '../snippets/'` imports
